sections.heading: Overview
sections.paragraph: The June 2026 pharmaceutical landscape highlights significant advancements in targeted protein degradation, led by Arvinas and Genovyn. Arvinas continues to expand its PROTAC platform beyond oncology, partnering with the Michael J. Fox Foundation to target LRRK2 for Parkinson’s disease via ARV-102, while its FDA-approved VEPPANU (vepdegestrant) for ER+/HER2- ESR1-mutated breast cancer transitions commercially through a transaction with Rigel Pharmaceuticals. The pipeline further includes ARV-806 for KRAS G12D cancers and ARV-393 for non-Hodgkin Lymphoma. Concurrently, Genovyn strengthened its AI-enabled discovery capabilities by appointing industry veterans Sammy Bell and Jim Edinger, leveraging Bell’s PROTAC expertise to integrate computational intelligence with translational execution. These developments occur amidst broader regulatory shifts, including US bipartisan scrutiny of biotechnology licensing deals involving China-based drugmakers and Australia’s 2026 Federal Budget allocating funds to research institutions. While regulatory oversight on human research protections remains stringent, the convergence of AI-driven discovery and established clinical programs underscores the maturing sector focused on targeting undruggable proteins across oncology and neurodegeneration.

points.text: Arvinas — VEPPANU — ER+/HER2- ESR1-mutated advanced/metastatic breast cancer — FDA approved PROTAC
Arvinas — ARV-102 — LRRK2 for neurodegenerative disorders (Parkinson’s) — Partnered with Michael J. Fox Foundation
Arvinas — ARV-806 — KRAS G12D for mutated cancers (pancreatic/colorectal) — Clinical development
Arvinas — ARV-393 — BCL6 for relapsed/refractory non-Hodgkin Lymphoma — Clinical development
Arvinas — ARV-027 — polyQ-AR for Spinal-Bulbar Muscular Atrophy — Clinical development
Genovyn — PROTACs — Unspecified — Leadership Appointment to accelerate AI-enabled therapeutic discovery
Arvinas: Pioneering PROTAC protein degrader platform for debilitating diseases; advancing VEPPANU and multiple investigational drugs.

Genovyn: Strategic objective to accelerate therapeutic discovery and development through integration of artificial intelligence, translational science, and precision biology, leveraging PROTAC expertise.
Pfizer: Partner on VEPPANU development and commercialization; transaction with Rigel for global rights.
Rigel Pharmaceuticals: Acquiring exclusive global rights to VEPPANU from Arvinas and Pfizer.
Michael J. Fox Foundation: Partnering with Arvinas on LITE and PPMI programs for LRRK2 therapies.
VEPPANU: Heterobifunctional protein degrader (PROTAC); FDA approved; ER+/HER2- ESR1-mutated advanced/metastatic breast cancer; disease progression after endocrine therapy.
ARV-102: Targets LRRK2; Neurodegenerative disorders (Parkinson’s); Collaboration with Michael J. Fox Foundation.
ARV-806: Targets KRAS G12D; Mutated cancers (pancreatic, colorectal).
ARV-393: Targets BCL6; Relapsed/refractory non-Hodgkin Lymphoma.
ARV-027: Targets polyglutamine-expanded androgen receptor (polyQ-AR); Spinal-Bulbar Muscular Atrophy (Kennedy’s disease).
Genovyn Platform: Integrates AI with translational science; CSO Dr. Bell has experience with targeted protein degraders.
Arvinas and Pfizer entered a transaction with Rigel Pharmaceuticals for exclusive global rights of VEPPANU; closing subject to consents/approvals including Hart-Scott-Rodino.
Arvinas joined Michael J. Fox Foundation’s LITE and PPMI programs to advance LRRK2 therapies.
Genovyn — Appointment of Sammy Bell (CSO) and Jim Edinger (CDO) to strengthen scientific, translational, and development capabilities across AI-driven platform.
